findingDetails.title: Detached CLI fan-out prompt omits required review init bindings
findingDetails.evidence: buildLoopPrompt omits review_target/review_target_type/review_dimensions/lineage_mode while deep_review_auto.yaml preflight requires those setup values before writes; cli-opencode passes that prompt directly as the run message.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add the same setup bindings used by buildNativeCommandInput to detached CLI review lineage prompts and add a regression assertion for step_preflight_contract coverage.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared buildLoopPrompt, buildLineageCommand, buildNativeCommandInput, and deep_review_auto.yaml preflight; issue is confined to detached CLI review lineage prompt setup.

findingDetails.title: Unrecoverable iteration salvage can still produce a fulfilled lineage when the summary report exists
findingDetails.evidence: runSalvageSweep increments failed after writing a fanout_salvage_failed marker for missing iteration markdown, but fanout-run only rejects timeout/non-zero exits, missing top-level expected artifacts, or stop-policy violations before returning fulfilled output; review expected artifacts only include review-report.md, and fanout-merge proceeds from registries.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reject or retry a lineage when salvage.failed > 0 unless every state-log iteration has a non-marker iteration markdown file; attach salvage summary to the thrown error so salvage_miss classification runs.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked fanout-salvage.cjs, fanout-run.cjs, fanout-pool.cjs, cli-guards.cjs, fanout-merge.cjs, and deep_review_auto.yaml; salvage_miss exists only for rejected worker errors and is not invoked for fulfilled outputs.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 adds weight 5 against two active P1 findings total weight 10; no refinement weight applied.

focus: runner retry/exit semantics around partial fan-out lineages and existing active P1s

findingDetails.title: Mixed salvage/missing-artifact failures skip the transient retry path
findingDetails.evidence: fanout-run converts missing expected artifacts into a thrown failure with salvage.failed forced to at least one while preserving salvage.salvaged; cli-guards only classifies salvage_miss when failed > 0 and salvaged === 0; fanout-pool retries only retryable normalized errors, so mixed partial salvage plus missing top-level artifact becomes fatal exit and is not retried.
findingDetails.recommendation: Classify any salvage.failed > 0 lineage-artifact failure as retryable unless an explicit fatal exit class is present, or attach a separate missingArtifacts/artifact_miss retry signal; add a regression where one iteration is salvaged but the top-level expected artifact remains missing and the retry succeeds.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked fanout-run.cjs, cli-guards.cjs, fanout-pool.cjs, fanout-salvage.cjs, and fanout-run/pool unit tests; tests cover pure salvage-miss retry and zero-salvaged failure-class rollup, but not mixed {salvaged: >0, failed: >0} missing-artifact failures.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 adds weight 5 against three active P1 findings total weight 15; no refinement weight applied.

Existing active P1s are compatible with the new retry-classification issue.

focus: security -- executor environment and sandbox trust boundaries for detached fan-out lineages

findingDetails.title: Detached OpenCode lineages run with prompt-only write isolation
findingDetails.evidence: cli-opencode fan-out uses opencode run with --dangerously-skip-permissions and no sandbox/workspace restriction; fanout-run documents that lineageDir-only write enforcement is prompt-based, and the generated prompt merely says to write outputs to lineageDir and not touch outside paths.
findingDetails.recommendation: Stop using dangerous permission bypass for detached cli-opencode review lineages unless an equivalent path-scoped sandbox is active; pass the strongest available sandbox/workdir flags, expose safe sandboxMode where supported, and make unsafe fallback opt-in with a fatal warning for review fan-out.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked buildLineageCommand, buildLoopPrompt, sandbox resolution, executor env filtering, config validation, tests, and the deep-review protocol; cli-opencode sandboxMode is rejected and protocol docs still describe a sandboxed CLI dispatch boundary.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 adds weight 5 against four active P1 findings total weight 20; no refinement weight applied.

traceabilityChecks.evidence: protocol references sandboxed CLI dispatch while fanout-run uses dangerous permission bypass for cli-opencode

focus: traceability -- child phase docs and named implementation surfaces for review fan-out requirements-to-code coverage

findingDetails.title: Fan-out adversarial playbook claims exit-0/no-artifact coverage, but the referenced regression never exercises that path
findingDetails.evidence: Phase 009 requires adversarial scenarios to name runnable regression tests that fail when the fixed bug returns, and the fan-out scenario specifically guards an exit-0/no-artifact lineage. The cited fanout-run test path uses writeFlakySalvageMissStubBinary, whose first attempt exits 1, while the exit-0 no-artifact helper is defined but unused by any grep-visible assertion.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add or repoint the regression to an exit-0/no-artifact case using the existing no-artifact stub, assert it is classified/retried/failed as documented, and update the playbook only after the exact bug-under-guard path is covered.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared parent/phase scope, phase 004 adversarial requirements, phase 005 pass-criteria scope, the 09--fanout playbook scenario, the named fanout-run.vitest.ts assertions, and the runner's current missing-artifact branch. The gap is traceability/test-evidence coverage, not a newly proven runtime failure, because fanout-run.cjs currently throws on missing expected artifacts after exit 0.

focus: maintainability -- stale parent/phase documentation, placeholder remnants, and follow-on change cost around remediation packets

findingDetails.title: 009 remediation parent is marked complete while its parent docs still contain scaffolds and pending child states
findingDetails.evidence: The 009 parent continuity says Phase complete and completion_pct 100, but the same parent spec retains template placeholders for priority, handoff criteria, scope, dependencies, deliverables, requirements, success criteria, risks, open questions, pending child rows, and TBD handoffs; its implementation summary and tasks remain scaffold defaults. Completed child phases 005 and 006 provide counterevidence that child work progressed, but that increases maintenance risk because the parent no longer reflects child state.
findingDetails.recommendation: Regenerate/finalize the 009 parent spec, tasks, and implementation summary from the completed child docs, replace every scaffold placeholder and pending phase-map row, and rerun metadata backfill so parent status matches child evidence.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared the 156 parent phase map, 009 parent spec/plan/tasks/summary, completed child 005/006 specs, and graph metadata; the stale state is concentrated in the 009 parent packet docs rather than the completed child specs.

focus: resource-map-coverage -- verify whether remediation and fan-out implementation surfaces are discoverable through resource maps, graph metadata, and parent packet key-file pointers

findingDetails.title: Parent and remediation discovery metadata omit the fan-out/remediation implementation surfaces operators must resume
findingDetails.evidence: The parent spec declares deep-loop runtime, workflow, and deep/speckit command implementation surfaces and phase 009 remediation scope, but parent derived.key_files only lists the goal plugin, reports, spec.md, and GPT routing docs. The 009 remediation metadata is marked complete while key_files only lists scaffold docs plus an unrelated benchmark workflow, and the 009 spec continuity key_files array is empty. Resource-map globbing found no parent or 009 remediation resource-map.md.
findingDetails.recommendation: Backfill parent and 009 discovery artifacts so key_files and/or resource maps include the deep-loop runtime fan-out files, deep-loop workflow files, deep/speckit command surfaces, and remediation child docs; regenerate graph metadata from finalized packet docs after the stale 009 parent is repaired.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared parent scope/key-file pointers, parent graph metadata, 009 remediation graph metadata/continuity, resource-map presence, and counterevidence from 003 runtime metadata. The 003 child exposes fanout-run.cjs and fanout-pool.cjs, but the assigned remediation parent and root packet discovery surfaces do not route operators there.

focus: observability -- fan-out progress, lineage status, and operator-visible evidence across review lineage execution and synthesis

findingDetails.title: Lag-ceiling observability events normalize to unknown status
findingDetails.evidence: fanout-pool emits lag_ceiling_exceeded and lag_ceiling_abort events with gauges/action data, but fanout-run statusForLedgerEvent does not map those event names before passing status to appendObservabilityEvent, so normalized observability-events.jsonl rows receive unknown status.
findingDetails.recommendation: Map lag_ceiling_exceeded and lag_ceiling_abort to typed warning/failed-or-retrying statuses and add a normalized observability event regression assertion.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked fanout-pool emitters, fanout-run status mapper and mirroring, shared observability envelope, and deep_review_auto synthesis status expectations; issue is limited to normalized status classification.

focus: synthesis-readiness -- leaf-only state and historical moved-path citations for synthesis/merge/report parsing

findingDetails.title: Leaf-only GLM lineage is skipped by registry-only fan-out merge
findingDetails.evidence: The moved GLM lineage intentionally has no local review registry (iteration-010.md:43), while fanout-merge filters merge inputs to lineages with a non-null registry before merging findings (fanout-merge.cjs:717). JSONL findingDetails are parseable but will not be included in registry-only fanout merge unless a reducer reconstructs the registry or merge falls back to JSONL.
findingDetails.recommendation: Before synthesis/merge, run the reducer to reconstruct the lineage registry from JSONL or teach fanout-merge.cjs to consume leaf-only review state when registry files are intentionally absent; then assert the merged output carries all active GLM findings.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Packet directory listing exposed no local registry, strategy records that absence as intentional, and the merge implementation gates review merge inputs on registry presence rather than JSONL findingDetails.
findingDetails.claim: A GLM lineage that has only JSONL/strategy/iteration artifacts will be skipped by the current registry-only fan-out merge, dropping its active findings from merged synthesis.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked runner and workflow contracts for JSONL parsing: fanout-run validates max-iterations state at fanout-run.cjs:514 and the skill contract requires iteration JSONL fields at SKILL.md:460, but merge code still selects only registry-backed lineages.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A separate orchestrator/reducer step may reconstruct the missing registry before merge; that would downgrade this to a sequencing caveat, but this leaf packet currently has no registry and dispatch forbids creating one here.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if synthesis demonstrably rebuilds deep-review-findings-registry.json from this lineage JSONL before invoking fanout-merge, or if merge consumes findingDetails directly when the registry is absent.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 adds weight 5 against seven prior active P1 findings (35), one prior active P2 advisory (1), and this new P1 (5), yielding 5/41 = 0.121951.
